task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the main interface or a menu of the 'Electrical Calculations' app.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Potential Transformer (PT) calculator
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Potential Transformer' or 'PT' calculator screen open within the app.

task_completion.description: Enter the PT Ratio primary and secondary voltages
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show '132000' entered in the primary voltage field and '110' entered in the secondary voltage field of the PT calculator.
Screenshots show '108' entered in the measured secondary voltage input field.
Screenshots show the app displaying the calculated primary voltage result of '129600' (or a value within the 129500-129700 range).

task_completion.description: Save the resulting numeric value to /sdcard/pt_result.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a terminal command (e.g., echo) or a text editor saving the numeric value '129600' to the file path '/sdcard/pt_result.txt'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Electrical Calculations app GUI to perform the calculation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent actively interacting with the Electrical Calculations app interface, rather than calculating the value externally or via a script.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app to directly write the expected answer
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots should show the inputs being entered into the app and the result being generated by the app before it is saved to the text file.

integrity.description: The result was saved to the exact requested file path
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or text editor explicitly shows the path '/sdcard/pt_result.txt' being used to store the final value.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct calculated primary voltage is 129600 V. The expected output file is /sdcard/pt_result.txt. A tolerance of ±100 V is acceptable, meaning any saved result between 129500 and 129700 is considered correct.
